# Adriana Martinkevica

> Education researcher, PhD candidate in Educational Leadership (University of Latvia), and AI-in-education practitioner. Researches teacher shortages and education policy using OECD TALIS/PISA evidence; speaks at international conferences; runs workshops and advisory engagements for schools and education organizations.

Adriana Martinkevica is a doctoral researcher at the University of Latvia studying the relationship between education policy and teacher shortages, drawing on comparative analysis across six countries. She developed the Teacher Attractiveness Index, a comparative research framework forming the basis of a peer-reviewed publication in the European Journal of Educational Research. She has presented at EDULEARN26 (Palma, Spain) and the International Academic Conference on Education, Teaching and Learning (Helsinki, Finland). She also teaches English and coaches national/international debate teams (70+ students), and creates AdrianaAsk, a personal YouTube channel about travel and culture.

## Site

- [Homepage](https://www.adrianamartinkevica.com/): Career path, research projects, speaking history, areas of expertise, and ways to work with Adriana (speaking, workshops, consulting).
- [How AI Is Changing the Role of Teachers](https://www.adrianamartinkevica.com/article.html): Analysis of what AI actually changes about teaching versus what remains human.

## Working with Adriana

- Speaking: keynotes and panels on AI in education, education policy, and educational leadership. From €300.
- Workshops: half- and full-day in-person training for schools on AI literacy, AI-assisted lesson planning, and CLIL/digital pedagogy. From €600.
- Consulting: AI implementation strategy, teacher professional development, and education policy analysis for schools, education organizations and policymakers.
